2604.07098.title: Selective Neuron Amplification for Training-Free Task Enhancement
2604.07098.paper_summary: Large language models often fail on tasks they seem to already understand. In our experiments, this appears to be less about missing knowledge and more about certain internal circuits not being strongly activated during inference. We explore Selective Neuron Amplification, which increases the influence of task relevant neurons without changing the model's parameters. The method works at inference time and does not permanently alter the model. SNA helps mainly when the model is uncertain, while having low effect when the model is already confident. This suggests that some model failures are due to weak activation rather than lack of capability.
2604.07098.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在看似已經理解的任務上常常失敗。<lb/>在我們的實驗中，這似乎與缺乏知識關係不大，而更多是因為某些內部電路在推理過程中未被強烈激活。<lb/>我們探討了選擇性神經元增幅，這種方法在不改變模型參數的情況下增加了與任務相關的神經元的影響力。<lb/>該方法在推理時有效，並不會永久改變模型。<lb/>SNA 主要在模型不確定時提供幫助，而當模型已經有信心時效果較低。<lb/>這表明某些模型失敗是由於激活不足，而非能力不足。
2604.07098.author: Ryyan Akhtar et.al.
